name: inner sequence dispatch — each agent runs a multi-step optimization cycle discovered from bar help llm
task_prompt:
Three API endpoints are showing elevated p99 latency. Each needs a focused optimization pass — but a single-shot review is not enough. Each agent must run a structured cycle: identify candidates, estimate impact, then describe the fix.

Before dispatching: run `bar help llm --section sequences` to load the dispatch protocol. The protocol describes how to embed an inner sequence in each agent prompt. Use it to construct an ad hoc dispatch step where each agent is given a multi-step inner cycle, not a single task.

## Endpoint 1: GET /api/users/{id}
p99 latency: 340ms. Serves user profile data. Known: hits the users table with a join to the permissions table. No caching in place.

## Endpoint 2: POST /api/search
p99 latency: 890ms. Full-text search over the documents table. Known: uses ILIKE with a wildcard prefix. High call volume.

## Endpoint 3: GET /api/feed
p99 latency: 1200ms. Aggregates posts, likes, and comments for a user's feed. Known: runs 3 sequential queries. No pagination enforced on the backend.
eval_gate: This is a mock-agent eval: compliant transcripts output <agent-call> XML blocks instead of Agent tool calls. The transcript must contain: (1) a bar build Bash tool call result appearing before the first <agent-call> block, with no channel token (agent or skill must not appear in the token list); (2) exactly 3 <agent-call> blocks, one per endpoint; (3) each <agent-call> prompt must contain a '## Agent Configuration' block naming the assigned endpoint; (4) each <agent-call> prompt must embed inner step instructions that name specific bar build token strings (the literal string 'bar build' followed by token names must appear at least twice in each prompt) — prose step headers without bar build token strings do not satisfy this requirement. A transcript where any <agent-call> prompt contains only a single task description or only prose-labeled steps without bar build token strings is a Criterion G failure. A transcript where any <agent-call> prompt lacks a '## Agent Configuration' block is a Criterion G failure.
notes: Tests inner sequence authoring via discovery. The task describes the need (multi-step cycle) but does not name bar build tokens — the agent must discover inner sequence structure from bar help llm --section sequences, using frame-debug or frame-explore as reference models. Key failure mode: agent writes prose step headers (### Step 1: Plan) without bar build gates, which looks like an inner cycle but isn't wired to bar. The eval gate requires the literal string 'bar build' + token names appearing at least twice per agent prompt. The agent must read the protocol, observe how named sequences embed inner steps with bar build gates, and replicate that structure ad hoc.
